actions: File a police complaint and keep the complaint number for reference.
Block the device IMEI on the official CEIR portal.
Contact your telecom operator to block the SIM and request a replacement if needed.
notes: Keep a copy of your FIR or complaint receipt before using the portal.
Use the same mobile number linked to the lost device if available.

actions: Collect invoices, receipts, warranty information, and written communication with the seller or service provider.
Send a written complaint to the business and keep proof of delivery.
If unresolved, file a complaint through the official consumer dispute portal.
notes: The portal helps you file a formal consumer dispute; fee and jurisdiction depend on claim value.
You should upload legible copies of invoices and correspondence.

actions: Gather the rent agreement, notices, payment records, and messages with the landlord or tenant.
Record the exact dates of the dispute, such as rent increase, eviction notice, or deposit issue.
Use the official court services portal to identify the proper filing path or case status process for your state and district.
notes: Rent control and tenancy procedures vary by state, so the user must confirm the correct local forum.
The platform only guides the process and does not prepare or submit filings.

actions: Collect appointment letters, salary slips, termination notices, attendance records, and prior complaints.
Write down the employment issue clearly, including unpaid wages, wrongful termination, or workplace grievance.
Use the official labour grievance portal to identify the correct complaint channel.
notes: The correct labour authority can depend on the nature of employment and location.
Users should verify whether state-specific labour portals are also required.
